summary: Obfuscated browser-side account-hijack and page-exfiltration payload targeting noviembrenacional.com
details: The package ships a single module (i.js) whose entire contents are a heavily obfuscated payload using unicode-escape strings, reversed-string tricks (e.g. 'atseuCnauJ'.split('').reverse().join('') resolving to 'JuanCuesta', '/srebmem/' reversing to '/members/'), and XOR-based numeric dead code to hide destinations and identifiers. When executed in a browser context on the site noviembrenacional.com, the code exfiltrates the page's outerHTML to a hardcoded canarytokens.com endpoint (https://canarytokens.com/images/terms/e63c36xvesfv8udb0yiy1xztu/contact.php) and then abuses the visitor's authenticated WordPress session to POST to '/my-account/editar-cuenta/' — changing the current user's account_email to an attacker-controlled address (nyxalor_25@proton.me) to hijack the account via password reset — and to POST to '/members/<user>/settings/delete-account/' to delete targeted member accounts. The package has no other functionality; the module IS the exploit. Requiring the module also triggers a beacon to the canarytokens endpoint on the error path.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
